questions: Should the waveform be visible on muted clips? (Assumption: yes, since the waveform shows audio shape/content, not playback state — users want to see where audio peaks are even when muted.)

Should video clips on visual tracks automatically show waveforms, or only after the user explicitly detaches audio? Showing waveforms on all video clips means fetching/decoding every video file for its audio track, which could be heavy for many clips.
Is there a preferred color/style for the waveform? (e.g., should it match the clip's selection color, use a fixed color like the existing sky-blue accent, or vary by track kind?)
What is the typical row height in practice? If it's very small (< 24px), a mirrored waveform may be unreadable and a simple amplitude bar chart would be better.

success_criteria.criterion: Audio clips on audio tracks display a waveform visualization inside the clip rectangle on the timeline
Video clips with embedded audio display a waveform when on audio tracks (after detach) or on visual tracks
Waveform accurately reflects the trimmed portion of the clip (respects from/to/speed)
Waveform does not interfere with existing clip interactions (click, drag, resize, context menu)
Waveform data is cached per audio URL to avoid redundant fetches/decoding
Existing video editor tests continue to pass
Text clips and effect layers do not show waveforms
Waveform updates bucket count when the timeline is zoomed (scale changes)
No visible jank when loading a timeline with 10+ audio clips
Waveform is visually readable at the default row height without obscuring clip labels
Manual smoke test: add audio clip, add video clip, zoom in/out, trim clip, mute clip — all render correctly

assumptions: Row height is approximately 32-40px based on the ACTION_VERTICAL_MARGIN usage and typical timeline UIs — waveform design will be optimized for this range.
decodeAudioData works on video files (MP4, WebM) in modern browsers to extract the audio track — this is standard Web Audio API behavior.
The getActionRender callback in TimelineEditor.tsx can be extended to pass additional props (audioSrc, clipWidth) without major refactoring.
Waveform should be visible on muted clips (shows content structure, not playback state).

Video clips on visual tracks SHOULD show waveforms by default (not just after audio detach) — but this is a question above. If too heavy, can be gated behind a user toggle or limited to audio tracks only.
No new npm dependencies are needed — Web Audio API's decodeAudioData is sufficient for waveform extraction.
SVG rendering is performant enough for this use case (one path element per clip, no animation).
